beats.activity: Wake up, shower, strong coffee in the kitchen
beats.reasoning: Felix wakes early on a clear day; the heat forecast and air quality from wildfire smoke means he'll want to get outdoor time in before peak afternoon. Starting at home in Wallingford.

beats.activity: Drive to the studio in Fremont
beats.reasoning: Commute from Wallingford to Fremont studio; 35 min accounts for clear morning traffic on a Wednesday.

beats.activity: First tattoo client session; detailed geometric/botanical design
beats.reasoning: 3+ hour morning session is typical for Felix's detailed work; he's hyper-focused and takes pride in clean lines. Studio is cool and climate-controlled, ideal as heat builds outside.

beats.activity: Lunch at Whole Foods Market, grab salad and cold drink
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch walk from studio; staying hydrated and eating light given the 91F heat forecast. Fremont location is close by and familiar.

beats.activity: Browse Elliott Bay Book Company, search for foraging or nature books
beats.reasoning: Felix can't resist a bookstore and has a nostalgia for Pioneer Square; 45 min drive from Fremont is a generous buffer. Indoors beats the heat and smoke; this fits his reflective mood from yesterday.

beats.activity: Drive back to studio in Fremont
beats.reasoning: Return commute; 45 min accounts for midday traffic and the route back to Fremont.

beats.activity: Afternoon tattoo session and studio admin; sketch new ideas
beats.reasoning: 3.5-hour afternoon block covers a second client session plus downtime for sketching. Studio stays cool; Felix uses this time to plan weekend foraging trip or work on design concepts.
Evening commute from studio back to Wallingford; 45 min accounts for rush-hour traffic on a Wednesday.

beats.activity: Quick dinner at home; hydrate and rest before evening
beats.reasoning: Felix eats at home to recover from the heat and smoke exposure. He's intentionally skipping the Gas Works Park concert (too crowded, air quality is moderate)—fits his preference for intimate time over big crowds.

beats.activity: Read new foraging book, listen to music, wind down for the evening
beats.reasoning: Felix settles into a quiet evening at home, continuing the reflective mood from yesterday. Reads the book he picked up and lets the day settle. This is his wind-down beat before sleep.
Felix gets a solid 7.75-hour sleep, ending just after 6am the next morning. The day's heat and smoke exposure warrant good rest for recovery.

diary: Woke up early with that pre-summer energy—91 degrees by afternoon, so I knew it'd be a scorcher. Strong coffee in Wallingford, then the drive to First Hill. My morning client was into geometric botanicals, really intricate stuff. Those sessions are meditative, you know? Grabbed a salad at Whole Foods after, then wandered Elliott Bay looking for foraging books. Found this gorgeous one on wild edibles—been thinking about exploring that side of things lately. Afternoon was solid too, sketched some new designs between clients. The heat really kicked in, so I hydrated like crazy. Heard about the free concert at Gas Works Park tonight, but honestly felt pretty wiped. Stayed home, read the new book with some music, let the evening cool down. There's something nice about choosing stillness when the city's buzzing.
thought_process: The heat made me think about pacing—I could've pushed to the concert, but I listened to what my body needed after two solid client sessions. The foraging book purchase wasn't random; I've been craving something grounded, literally and figuratively. Work felt purposeful today, especially sketching new ideas in the afternoon slump. The smoke from BC wildfires was subtle but noticeable, made me more aware of hydration and my breathing. I think I'm shifting toward valuing quiet evenings over FOMO—the concert will happen, but reading at home felt like the right call. Small choice, but it felt intentional.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
